rulings.Case Title: VERLIANT ENERGY, INC. VS. DAKOTA NOTE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel Re First Amended Complaint (C.C.P. 128.7)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 16, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT DAKOTA NOTE, LLC's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel Re First Amended Complaint (C.C.P. 128.7) DENIED. Insufficient evidence presented for the Court to conclude that this action was brought or is being pursued in violation of CCP 128.7(b)(1) or (3). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BLACK CREEK BUILDERS, INC VS. MISTER JIU'S LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion To Expunge Plaintiff'S Notice Of Pendency Of Action And Request For Judical Notice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 16, 2019 line 2. Defendants' Motion To Expunge Plaintiff'S Notice Of Pendency Of Action And Request For Judical Notice GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 405.22 and 405.23. While failure to comply with one requirement may not have invalidated the Notice of Pendency, there are too many technical mistakes with recordation of this/these Notices. The Court makes no ruling on the validity of the real property claim at this time. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $5,520. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MOISES MONGALO ET AL VS. WALTER WORSHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 16, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT MARSHALL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. The Court has previously warned the Defendant to only file the ANTI-SLAPP if appropriate. Defendant filed this motion without providing any explanation for its decision to not file a timely ANTI-SLAPP or providing any basis for the Court to exercise its discretion to allow a late filing. Additionally, while Defendant's motion heavily relies on Sutter, decided prior to significant change in ANTI-SLAPP law, there is no discussion of Baral and its progeny on applicability of Sutter. Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. The Court does not suggest that Sutter is no longer good law. However, Sutter's reliance on "gravamen" of the cause of action warranted a discussion. See Rutter-Guide, Civil Procedure Before Trial 7:772. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRENT MARSH VS. MICHAEL CAMPESINO ET AL

DEFENDANT MICHAEL CAMPESINO's Motion For Attorneys' Fees In Contract Action continued to August 2, 2019 for Plaintiff to provide copies of the attorney time entries (with appropriate redactions) no later than July 26, 2019. = (501/CFH)
